title: Expressions, Control Flow, Iteration, Switches, and Deferred Work · Runtime Recall
summary: A retry policy must classify states, bound attempts, accumulate evidence, and guarantee cleanup across early returns. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a bounded retry and classification engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace arithmetic, comparison, logical, bitwise, shift, precedence, conversion, and evaluation-order guarantees from concrete values.
Use if statements with optional initializers and narrow variable scope around the decision they support.
Build bounded for loops and range loops while reasoning about copied iteration values, indexes, order, and Go 1.22 loop variables.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
